what_it_does: Documents the headless-control contract entry: the stream-json protocol layer of a programmatic Claude Code session, mapping native claude -p events and control frames to the company's supervisor-folded per-session frames and durable events, and cataloging which slices are exposed, internal, or planned.
observations: Defines three vocabularies: native stream-json events, supervisor-folded per-session frames, and durable agent_sessions event log.
Native events include system/init, assistant text/tool_use, result, system/api_retry, and stream_event partial deltas.
The supervisor spawn hardcodes --input-format stream-json and --output-format stream-json and does not request --include-partial-messages.
headless-control.watch is the same endpoint as session.watch and emits folded ndjson frames, not the raw native stream.

headless-control.act exposes turn injection via /inject and interrupt via /interrupt; set-output partially threads --output-format and --include-partial-messages but --json-schema structured output is still planned.
The interrupt op is unproven against a real Claude turn because stubs ignore control_request.
Choosing output_format=text or json on a held-open supervised session produces a shape the supervisor reader cannot fold; the contract directs consumers to one-shots instead.
Error codes include headless-control.session-busy (409), headless-control.output-not-selectable (501), and inherited session.unknown (404).
The planned json output carries a CC-20 total_cost_usd telemetry seam.
Status is building and the entry relates to session, events, permission, model, and agent-team.
standouts: Explicit honest-gap labeling: api_retry and partial-message deltas are not folded, interrupt is unproven, and json_schema structured output is unbuilt.
The resource is framed as real, not fictional: the company's session supervisor already runs this protocol.
watch and act are retrieval lenses over existing session ops rather than separate endpoints.
Built-but-not-live-verified: output_format and include_partial flag threading is unit-tested on the command builder but not confirmed with a real spawn.
conventions: Contract-entry front matter with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, and relates-to.
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, contract:error, and contract:example fenced markers.
Status-aware language: building, planned, unverified, exposed, internal.
Atlas references (CC-18.x) and verification metadata blocks.
concerns: Several forward-looking capabilities are described as planned inside a contract (json_schema, --bare, text/json output on held-open sessions).
Interrupt is exposed via /interrupt despite being unproven against real Claude behavior.
The /spawn binding allows output_format choices that the file itself says will break the supervisor's fold.
No formal JSON schema is provided for the folded frame vocabulary beyond the prose table.
notes: A protocol contract that is unusually honest about what the supervisor currently exposes, what it swallows, and what remains unbuilt or unproven.
questions: What is the exact JSON shape of the folded init frame if model and tools are omitted?
How does the supervisor map the native claude session_id to the as-<hex> handle?
What does the control_request interrupt frame look like on the wire?
Where is COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S defined and what is its value?
How will --json-schema be wired when output_format is stream-json versus json?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document the headless control protocol and the company's exposed slice; the file does exactly that, with explicit gap annotations.
apparent_themes: Protocol layering: native vs folded vs durable vocabularies.
Supervisor as the sole parent process; consumers never hold raw pipes.
Incremental exposure of native Claude Code headless features.
intention_signals: To give UI/API consumers a precise, self-aware map of which headless control capabilities are reachable through the fabric today and which still require direct claude -p usage or future work.
novelty: It treats a third-party CLI protocol as a first-class contract resource and explicitly distinguishes native events, supervisor-folded frames, and durable events as three separate vocabularies.
contribution: A structured, gap-aware contract that records the mapping between the native Claude Code stream-json protocol and the company's supervisor/session abstractions, including honest status labels for unexposed, unverified, and planned slices.
summary_for_embedding: Contract entry for the headless-control resource describing the stream-json protocol of a programmatic Claude Code session, the supervisor's fold into per-session frames, and the exposed versus planned control-input and output-stream operations.
intention_for_embedding: To document which headless control protocol slices the company's session supervisor re-exposes, keeps internal, or has not yet built, so consumers know what they can and cannot drive through fabric faces.
